---
title: "Day 0: Pack your backpack"
author: "Nguyen MTS, Quan TNM"
date: "2026-05-11"
---

The purpose of this pre-workshop day is to prepare you with the computing environment where we will practice genomic analysis and the essential skills to work effectively in this environment.

Our environment is a cloud-based Linux system with a set of pre-installed tools for the workshop, so you won't have to use your computer's resource for the analyses. 

In the sub-module [0.1: Setup your workspace](setup-environment.qmd), you will be guided to prepare your laptop and access this system from your own computer.

The setup guide is split into short pages so you can open only the section you need:

- [Setup overview](setup-environment.qmd)
- [Windows 10/11 setup](setup-windows.qmd)
- [macOS setup](setup-macos.qmd)
- [Ubuntu setup](setup-ubuntu.qmd)
- [Optional graphical bioinformatics apps](setup-bioinformatics-apps.qmd)
- [Workshop server access](setup-server-access.qmd)
- [Verification checklist](setup-verification.qmd)
- [Troubleshooting and FAQs](setup-troubleshooting-faq.qmd)
- [Advanced R in VS Code](setup-r-vscode.qmd)

In the sub-module [0.2 Commands & Environments](linux-basics.qmd), we will learn about Linux operating system (OS) and the essential skills to run bioinformatic tools on it. After finishing this module, please take a quick [Knowledge Check](final-quiz.qmd) (10 mins) to test your Linux skills.
